title: Aragallus parryi
taxonomy: Aragallus parryi is a rare species of flowering plant in the legume family. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it grows in sandy or gravelly soils. Aragallus parryi is classified as a member of the tribe Loteae, which also includes the lotus and lotus tree. This plant is further classified within the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. The classification of Aragallus parryi also includes its genus, which is a small group of plants related to the common lotus.

intro: Aragallus parryi, also known as Parry's agallia or Parry's prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to the Great Plains region of North America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, fever, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Aragallus parryi include isoflavones, phenolic acids, and other compounds that contribute to its medicinal properties.
